sections.paragraph:
ἐπακούω fut. ἐπακούσομαι; 1 aor. ἐπήκουσα; pf. 3 sg. ἐπακήκοε LXX; inf. ἐπακηκοέναι (Just., D. 137, 4); 1 aor. pass. 3 sg. ἐπηκούσθη 2 Ch 30:27 (s. ἀκούω; Hom. [Il. 2, 143 in the sense ‘hear about’]+; Tat. 30, 2 ‘understand’ a foreign tongue). On LXX s. JBarr, JTS 31, ’80, 67–72.

① to pay close attention to what one is told w. implication of being responsive, hear, listen to (Aeschyl., Choëph. 725; Lucian, Tim. 34 τ. εὐχῶν; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 478, 6=BGU 1080, 6; IMaronIsis 7; PGM 13, 207 μοι; LXX; τῆς φωνῆς ἡμῶν En 103:14; 106:8; Jos., Ant. 9, 10) τινός someone (Lucian, Pseudolog. 23; UPZ 78, 24 [159 b.c.] Isis, ἐπάκουσόν μου; PGM 3, 497; Gen 17:20 al.; TestAbr A 14 p. 94, 2 [Stone p. 36]; JosAs 14:2) 1 Cl 8:3 (quot. of unknown orig.; cp. Is 65:24); B 3:5; ἐπήκουσά σου 2 Cor 6:2 (Is 49:8; CCox, Biblica 62, ’81, 251–58); τῆς δεήσεως GJs 2:4; 4:1f; 20:3 (last not pap); προσευχομένου Ἰωνᾶ AcPlCor 2:30.—OWeinreich, Θεοὶ ἐπήκοοι: MAI 37, 1912, 1–68.

② to act appropriately in response to what one hears, heed, obey (Hes., Op. 275; Hdt. 4, 141; Pla., Soph., 227c; Vett. Val. 67, 16; Judg 2:17 A; 1 Macc 10:38 v.l.) abs. πρέπον ἐστὶν ἐ. κατὰ μηδεμίαν ὑπόκρισιν it is fitting to be obedient without hypocrisy IMg 3:2. ἔμπροσθέν τινος before someone = obey someone B 12:11 (Is 45:1).—New Docs 1, 15. M-M. TW. Spicq. Sv.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

IMaronIsis IMaronIsis = Une nouvelle arétalogie d’Isis à Maronée—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

orig. orig. = original(ly)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Biblica Biblica (unabbr.)—List 6

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

MAI MAI = Mitteilungen des Deutschen Archäologischen Instituts—Lists 3, 6

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

abs. abs. = absolute

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 358
